title: go get github.com/containers/podman/v3/pkg/bindings returns an error
body:
**Is this a BUG REPORT or FEATURE REQUEST? (leave only one on its own line)**

/kind bug

**Description**
Running ```go get github.com/containers/podman/v3/pkg/bindings``` returns the following error:
```
go get github.com/containers/podman/v3/pkg/bindings: module github.com/containers/podman@upgrade found (v1.9.3), but does not contain package github.com/containers/podman/v3/pkg/bindings
```
This is on a Fedora 33, up-to-date.

**Steps to reproduce the issue:**

1. Follow the doc nested in the pkg/bindings

2.

3.

**Describe the results you received:**
The module doesn't seem to be published or something.

**Describe the results you expected:**
It should install the module as documented.

**Additional information you deem important (e.g. issue happens only occasionally):**

**Output of `podman version`:**

```
podman version 2.2.1
```

**Output of `podman info --debug`:**


comments.body: Already fixed in main. But not yet released.  Will be fixed with v3.0.2.
Since it's in the main branch, you can do `go get github.com/containers/podman/v3/pkg/bindings@master` until v3.0.2 is out.
I am closing it since it's fixed in the main branch.
